reasoning: The document has strong text preservation (shingling recall 0.987), but it contains a MAJOR structural defect where a running header interrupts a sentence spanning a page boundary. This disrupts the logical reading order and requires a human reviewer to resolve. Several minor issues related to header/footers and standalone links were also noted.

concerns.analysis: The output contains running header text at the start of Page 2 ('FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL...') that interrupts a sentence spanning the page boundary. Page 1 ends with 'Persons wishing to', and Page 2 continues the sentence with 'comment on the proposed permit...', but the repeated header is injected between them in the reading order as regular body headings and paragraphs, breaking the logical reading flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1: "Persons wishing to" -> Page 2: "comment on the proposed permit..."
concerns.output_quote: {"type": "paragraph", "text": "The public may also submit written comments... Persons wishing to"} followed by {"type": "heading", "level": 1, "text": "FACT SHEET"} ... followed by {"type": "paragraph", "text": "comment on the proposed permit..."}
concerns.location: End of Page 1 / Start of Page 2

concerns.description: Page number artifacts retained: Page 1 of 2
Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer elements are present in extraction data. This is common in multi-page government PDFs and is typically cosmetic — the renderer strips them from HTML output.

action_items: Remove the running header from the middle of the sentence spanning Pages 1 and 2 to restore logical reading order
Ensure hyperlinks are inline within paragraphs rather than standalone blocks
Remove any remaining page number or header/footer artifacts
